name: Sklearn Ridge Regression
description: Ridge Regression solves a linear regression problem by minimizing the least squares loss while applying L2 regularization to the model coefficients. This helps reduce model variance, handle multicollinearity, and improve generalization on unseen data.

fields.name: targetCol
fields.title: Target Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous numeric value that the Ridge Regression model learns to predict.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: List of input feature columns used to train the regression model. All features must be numeric, boolean, or vector-compatible.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Ratio used to split the dataset into training and testing subsets. A value of 1.0 uses the entire dataset for training.

fields.name: alpha
fields.title: Alpha
fields.description: Regularization strength applied to the L2 penalty term. Higher values increase shrinkage of coefficients and reduce model complexity.

fields.name: fitintercept
fields.title: Fit Intercept
fields.description: Determines whether an intercept term is included in the regression model. Disabling this assumes data is already centered.

fields.name: normalize
fields.title: Normalize
fields.description: If enabled and fit_intercept is true, input features are normalized before regression, which can improve numerical stability.

fields.name: maxiter
fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of iterations for iterative solvers such as lsqr or sparse_cg to reach convergence.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Convergence tolerance for the solver. Training stops when updates fall below this threshold.

fields.name: solver
fields.title: Solver
fields.description: Algorithm used to solve the Ridge Regression optimization problem. Different solvers vary in speed, memory usage, and dataset suitability.

fields.name: randomstate
fields.title: Random State
fields.description: Seed controlling randomness in solvers that rely on data shuffling, ensuring reproducible model training results.
